steps.title: Identify the referenced event
steps.content: The passage specifies “the 1984 World's Fair cost \$350 million”—naming the exact event whose attendance we need to find in the table.

steps.title: Locate the attendance data from the table
steps.content:
The table shows the 1984 World's Fair had 7.35 million visitors—the figure that directly completes the sentence and supports the argument that fairs are “too expensive and not popular enough.” The other choices show attendance from different years.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
